zh.lifeSummary: 當Agnes de Walton於1228年出生在英國瓦維其可爾史達科特 奧 阿沃時，她的父親Simon de Walton - Bishop of Norwich31歲，母親Agnes23歲。她於1280年之前與James Shirley結婚。他們至少是8個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世在英國瓦維其可爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Agnes de Walton于1228年出生在英国瓦維其可爾史達科特 奧 阿沃时，她的父亲Simon de Walton - Bishop of Norwich31岁，母亲Agnes23岁。她于1280年之前与James Shirley结婚。他们至少是8个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世在英国瓦維其可爾。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Walton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places called Walton, which are named with Old English walh ‘foreigner, Briton, serf’ (genitive plural wala), wald ‘woodland’, wall ‘wall’, or wælla ‘well, spring, stream’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.

History: George Walton (1741–1804) signed the Declaration of Independence. He was born in Prince Edward County, VA, whither his grandfather had emigrated from England in 1682. He moved to Savannah, GA, and became governor of GA and a prominent jurist.

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Agnes de Walton was born in 1228, in Stratford on Avon, Warwickshire, England, her father, Simon de Walton - Bishop of Norwich, was 31 and her mother, Agnes, was 23. She married James Shirley before 1280. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 1 daughter. She died in Warwickshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Agnes de Walton est née en 1228, à Stratford on Avon, Warwickshire, Angleterre, son père, Simon de Walton - Bishop of Norwich, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Agnes, avait 23 ans. Elle a épousé James Shirley avant 1280. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée à Warwickshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Agnes de Walton 1228 in Stratford on Avon, Warwickshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Simon de Walton - Bishop of Norwich 31 und ihre Mutter Agnes 23. Sie hat vor 1280 James Shirley geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 8 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb in Warwickshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes de Walton è nata il nel 1228 nel Stratford on Avon, Warwickshire, England, sua padre, Simon de Walton - Bishop of Norwich, aveva 31 anni e sua madre, Agnes, aveva 23 anni. Lei sposato James Shirley prima del 1280. Erano genitori di almeno 8 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel Warwickshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Agnes de Waltonが1228年にイギリスワーウィクシュアイアストラトフォード オン アボンで生まれた時，父Simon de Walton - Bishop of Norwichは31歳，母Agnesは23歳であった。1280年以前にJames Shirleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも8人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女はイギリスワーウィクシュアイアで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes de Walton(이)가 1228년 영국 워윅실르 스트라트퍼드  아폰에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Simon de Walton - Bishop of Norwich(은)는 31세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Agnes(은)는 23세였다. 그녀는 1280년 이전 James Shirley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 워윅실르에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes de Walton nasceu em 1228, em Stratford on Avon, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Simon de Walton - Bishop of Norwich, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Agnes, tinha 23 anos. Ela casou-se com James Shirley antes 1280. Eles tiveram pelo menos 8 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em Warwickshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Agnes de Walton родилась в 1228 г. в местности стратфорд он авон, варвиккшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Simon de Walton - Bishop of Norwich было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Agnes, 23. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени James Shirley перед 1280 г. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 1 дочь. Она умерла в местности варвиккшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Agnes de Walton nació en 1228, en Stratford on Avon, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Simon de Walton - Bishop of Norwich, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Agnes, tenía 23 años. Se casó con James Shirley antes de 1280. Fueron padres de por lo menos 8 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en Warwickshire, Inglaterra.
